w:r.w:t: Pvt Ltd. reserves the right to amend any portion of this document at any time. This document is marked as confidential and provided for internal use in connection with
services only. All information contained in the document is proprietary
and confidential and all rights therein are reserved with
Pvt Ltd. Client shall not disclose this document to any third party other than the intended receiver. Use of this document is subject to the terms and conditions in force with
expressly acknowledges and accepts this disclaimer.
Business case for Accessing the application

Founded in 1997, with industry strength and experience in the financial and banking sector. We develop world-class banking solutions - primarily focused on effective implementation of the

process, cutting down on operating expenses, reducing processing time and increasing customer satisfaction multi-fold. We have expanded into the financial services software arena, with the same dedication to excellence to be a touchstone of the corporate culture. We do not simply produce software. we create innovative solutions that allow the customers to outperform their competitors.

The documentation team along with Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) have contributed towards the creation of this document. This document has been a team effort with each member providing their inputs and suggestions for improvement so that the document meets End-User requirements. The team consists of Senior Technical Writer, Subject Matter Expert and unnamed countless other members who have made this approach document possible.
The normal font used in the document is Cambria.
The main pages are divided into chapters. Each chapter starts with Heading 1.
The sub-pages are further divided into sections and subsections are represented as Heading 1.1, through Heading 1.1.1.1.1
The steps involved to navigate through the application are provided in Bullet points. Sometimes the bullet points may have sub-levels of steps. Each level will display different Bullet points.
The information that needs emphasis is in Bold Fonts. It may represent the page name, field name, drop-down, checkbox, radio buttons, links, or action buttons.
The content “Bolded and inserted within Quotes” represents the pre-set options or list values that you may select from one of the various control fields. E.g.: “Yes”, “No”.
A note inserted in the document at relevant places is represented with (bulb icon) at relevant locations.
Any References will be represented with (book icon) at relevant locations.
Any Warning message is represented with (alert icon) at relevant locations.
Any Alert messages are represented with (alert icon) at relevant locations.
Any Hyperlink is represented in with normal font in blue color, bolded, underlined and italicized and displays as “
This document outlines the proposed solution for implementing MSME functionalities in the
system. It has been prepared based on discussions with the

A gap analysis was conducted to understand the bank’s current lending systems and processes. The findings from this analysis have been documented and referred to while preparing this Software Requirement Specification (SRS) document. Based on the approval of this document and further clarifications from the bank,
This section explains bank users accessing of
application strictly via the internal bank network through a URL provided by the
team. The system supports secure user login using existing credentials, validated against the bank’s authentication database.
with IP logging and session tracking
, validated via OTP sent to registered mobile and email
with OTP-based verification and password policy enforcement (minimum 8 characters,
numeric, and at least one uppercase character)
These features are fully aligned with the bank’s IT security. They ensure secure access while empowering users to manage their credentials independently.
As per bank guidelines, applications can be accessed only through the bank’s internal website. The Bank
team will provide the URL, which needs to be configured by the development team.
Users must manually enter the approved URL provided by the
The login screen allows authorized branch users to access the

system. Users can log in using their existing user ID and password. Upon entering their credentials, the system will validate the details and grant access if the entered information is correct. Otherwise, the system will display an error.
The user enters the approved URL in the browser and accesses the login screen.
The system validates the entered credentials against the bank’s authentication database.
If the credentials are correct, access is granted.
The user is redirected to the
the User ID or Password due to incorrect credentials.
The user attempts login with an
“Invalid credentials. Please try again, with valid credentials.”
If the login attempt is wrong for 3 times for invalid password, then account gets locked, user needs to contact administrator manually for unlocking.
The system locks the users in accessing the application if the tries for set configured times.
– The user inputs their current password for authentication.
– The user types in a new password, following security guidelines.
– The user re-enters the new password to avoid mistakes.
– The system processes the password change request.
– If the user does not wish to proceed, they can exit the screen.
matches the one stored in the system.
meets security policies (e.g., length, special characters, etc.).
If all conditions are met, update the password and show a
If any condition fails, display an
(e.g., "Passwords do not match" or "Invalid old password").
Ensure the new password meets the
. As per the Bank policy minimum of 8 characters and password should contain Alfa numeric case with 1 character in upper case.
A 6-digit OTP code will be generated by
and sent to the bank user's registered mobile number and email ID
206679 is the One Time Password (OTP) for your Change Password. Do not share OTP with anyone. Bank never calls to verify your OTP. –
We have received a request to reset the password for your
Please use the following One-Time Password (OTP) to proceed with resetting your password:
This OTP is valid for [X minutes] only. Do not share this OTP with anyone.
If you did not initiate this request, please contact your system administrator or the
User enters correct username and old password
User enters a new password meeting policy (min 8 characters,
System accepts the password and sends OTP to registered mobile/email
User enters correct OTP within valid time
New password and confirm password do not match
New password does not meet security policy
System detects invalid or expired OTP
Error: "OTP expired, please request new one"
User tries more than allowed attempts
Error: "Too many failed attempts. Try later."
button sends an OTP to the user's registered mobile number.
– The user inputs the received OTP.
If valid, the user proceeds to reset the password.
If invalid, an error message is displayed.
– If the OTP is not received or has expired, the user can click
– The user types in a new password, ensuring it meets security policies.
– The user re-enters the new password to avoid mistakes.
updates the password if all validations passed.
Ensure the new password meets the
. As per the Bank policy minimum of 8 characters and password should contain Alfa numeric case with at least 1 character in upper case.
If successful, the system updates the password and displays a
A 6-digit OTP code will be generated by
and sent to the bank user's registered mobile number and email ID
XXXXX is the One Time Password (OTP) for your Forget Password. Do not share OTP with anyone. Bank never calls to verify your OTP. –
We have received a request to reset the password for your
Please use the following One-Time Password (OTP) to proceed with defining New Password:
minutes] only. Do not share this OTP with anyone.
If you did not initiate this request, please contact your system administrator or the
User enters correct username and old password
User enters a new password meeting policy (min 8 characters,
System accepts the password and sends OTP to registered mobile/email
User enters correct OTP within valid time
New password and confirm password do not match
New password does not meet security policy
System detects invalid or expired OTP
Error: "OTP expired, please request new one"
User tries more than allowed attempts
Error: "Too many failed attempts. Try later."
Screen page after Successful login. All the four modules with
system is displayed. Ease of Access to application user to go directly to proposal they are working on recently.
Recent Applications list down last three applications that the user had recently worked on, based on the application opened/accessed by respective login user from date stamp logic.
Circular details / Helpdesk number will be based on Bank definition from Setup.
Dashboard is quick access; this page will be enabled for Users having Group Rights (Supervisory Grade) to see the overall applications and status.
A set of users will be grouped under defined User Groups (e.g., Processing Officer, Approver, Admin, etc.).
Access to specific pages and functionalities within the system will be granted based on the group to which the user belongs.
Restricted from accessing approval workflows or administrative functions.
Each logo will redirect Users to respective modules to which the User has rights to access, controlled from Admin level.
, MSME, and Corporate) there will be running number that will capture the Location wise User Statistics towards usage of
system and it will be denoted with color logics as follows Approved Applications (Green) / Pending (Orange) / Rejection (red).
Left top menu will display the details of User logged in.
All the other navigation menu will as per access privileges of the user.
The software platform's home screen is divided into two main sections:
The Dashboard is common across all users. It provides quick access to:
: Displays applications assigned for user action.
: Displays queries or tasks pending user clarification or input.
This panel is designed to support user-based control aligned with the organizational verticals. It is further divided into:
: Contains high-level functions such as Reports and monitoring tools, typically assigned to supervisory or managerial roles.
Proposal" function. This ensures that the
can configure and restrict Left Panel access based on the
, enforcing secure and relevant navigation control for each role.
Same applies for the Other vertical. MLP officer cannot have access to create Agri Application.
The Inbox section is displayed in the center of the
screen. The following are the various inboxes to be incorporated for
For detailed explanation on the above inboxes,
screen displays various inboxes based on the accessibility rights of the logged-in user.

is configured with major inboxes through which the proposal application flows to-and-fro between various users/user groups starting from initial proposal creation till the loan sanction. The following are the various inboxes available:
Processing inbox allows the user to access the applications pushed from the
for processing the loan applications. The application is received into:

displays the list of proposals which the logged-in user is current working on. The user can find the details of each application by clicking on the corresponding Proposal No. The recent proposals created by the users are listed on top of the list based on the date when the proposal is created.
The following are the scenarios handled in the Inbox.
Once the user initiates new proposal creation by clicking the
, the corresponding proposal will be moved to the Inbox.
When the user picks the proposal from Group Inbox,
auto pushes the proposal to Inbox of the logged-in user.
If the proposal is directly forwarded from another user to the logged-in user, that proposal will be listed in the user inbox.
System Result: A new Proposal No.
is generated and appears at the top of Amit’s Inbox.
Forward the application from the workflow and approval page to the Next Hierarchy.
has forwarded to a workflow where MLP assistant manager group
When the MLP Assistant Manager logs in, they will see the proposal count as

in the Group Inbox after Amit has forwarded the application. (Here, there are multiple MLPs—for example, MLP Bangalore will only see applications related to MLP Bangalore. This is controlled through user-level configurations.
is automatically moved to Riya's individual Inbox.
Amit’s Forward the application from the workflow and approval page to the Next Hierarchy
will be able to process the application directly from the inbox.

If the branch officer can view the status of the proposal by navigating to the existing proposal search. Branch officer needs to search the proposal by parameters like Name, date of proposal creation, proposal number and other parameters.

System auto pushes the proposals directly assigned to the branch into the Group Inbox of branch. From the Group Inbox, the user can self-assign the proposal to work with. The self-assigned proposals will be pushed to the Inbox of the user.
the MLP- In charge will allocate the application to user directly same can be configured, MLP In charge officer should go to the workflow and approval page and forward the application to the
From the Group Inbox, the user can self-assign the proposal to work with. To self-assign the proposal, the user needs to click the corresponding
On the popup window, the user can either click
to receive the proposal in his inbox or click
to cancel the action, as per his choice.
inbox is available only for the supervising authority. The
Team Inbox allows the supervising authority to view the list of proposal pertaining to the group and the proposals of all the individual users belonging to the group.
The Team Inbox is split into the following two sections.
displays the proposals of all the individual users in a particular location.
displays all the proposals of the
On the left navigation bar, click
to search for the existing proposal
The below screen appears. Here, you can search the proposal based on
-wise search, location-based search, or date-wise search.
please refer to the grid below
Please find the attached common flow for creating the application
Once the application is created, the user will be
navigated directly to the application level
, once associated product. Once the application is created system locks the
and loads the application and user
On clicking the Dedupe button, the system triggers an API call to the Core Banking System (CBS) to check for potential customer matches.
If a match is found, the system displays the matched customer details along with their existing loan relationships.
Based on the result, the user can either:
Proceed with a fresh loan proposal, or
Link the application to the existing loan and propose accordingly.
relevant to the locked scheme will be displayed for the user to fill in the required details.
not be allowed to change the requested loan amount
Proposed loan amount can be modified but not greater the Requested amount.
The Requested loan amount can be changed and
within the applicable limit selected ES note.
For All cases the user are not permitted change the ES note and if so then application and create new application.
based on the initially requested loan amount
Sanctioned loan amount will be final computed value after capturing the entire data touch points hence system could not assess the ES note type in the application level.
On click on the create new application from the Home screen a page will be enabled as shown below.
Branch Officer-Initiated MSME Loan Application: Workflow Handling

wp:docPr.name: Picture 1
wp:docPr.descr:
A diagram of a process
AI-generated content may be incorrect.

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 1
pic:cNvPr.descr:
A diagram of a process
AI-generated content may be incorrect.

w:r.w:t: When a Branch Officer initiates an MSME loan application in
LOS, the system evaluates the processing path at the time of application creation based on delegation powers.
The system uses configured global variables to determine the appropriate processing level.
The routing decision is based on:
These values are mapped against the delegation of powers matrix to decide whether the application should be:
Processed at the Branch level, or
Branch Officer must complete all mandatory data inputs (borrower details, financials, inspection, security, etc.)
Branch Manager will receive the complete application and forward the application to MLP
MLP Head will be marked for the receiving the application in his Group Inbox
MLP officer will forward (from workflow and approval screen) the application to the MLP officer
The routing logic is triggered immediately upon application creation, and the processing path is locked based on loan amount values.
However, during the next workflow stage, the identified path can be
forwarded to the next for further review
, and the user may send the proposal to MLP even if it is currently at the Branch Manager review stage
During that stage, the user will have access to the full application and can complete the entire application before forwarding it to the next workflow, as configured in the system by the bank.
The system will dynamically enable or disable specific workflow roles based on the identified processing level during the application creation.

Once the application is created and if user change the ES note which is selected during the application the application creation cannot be changed, once the application is created the associated attribute or modules which are required will be loaded and user cannot change
. If user wants to change/modify they have rejected the application from the workflow and approval screen and recreate the application with proper ES note selection.
This section enables users to capture customer-related information.
Details of the individual pages under this section are explained in the respective subsections.
This screen enables users to capture the customer details, add co-borrower/Guarantor to the application along with the External API checks

w:r.w:t: On clicking the 'Customer 360' button on the customer page, a popup window will be displayed listing the APIs based on the type of customer.
This screen enables the user to
, for all participants in the proposal, such as:
where a co-applicant or guarantor needs to be added
enablement and scale of this feature
. Process of same will be explained in detail in the workflow page.
Multiple credit report options (CIBIL, CRIF, Experian) are available. When the 'Get Report' button is clicked:
1. The associated API is triggered to the respective CIC vendors.
2. The credit report is generated and displayed
Scenario adding the co-applicant/guarantor who is Existing to Bank
Users can click on the add new customer button on the customer detail bottom of the page as shown below.
will appear, prompting the user to begin the customer addition process.
The user will be asked whether the customer has an
, the system will prompt the user to
On Click on the Search icon associated next to the CIF ID field API will be triggered, if the API is successful, then the data will be auto populated in the customer details section as shown below.
Before saving the customer detail section user needs to mandatorily select the type of borrower
tag the relationship with the borrower. As shown below
User enters CIF Number and clicks on Retrieve
CIF number is invalid or not mapped to customer.
Any API failure user needs to re
onboard the co-applicant and guarantor who is ETB.
is on hold, and the Bank will confirm post internal discussions and approvals.
Please find the attached field listing for Customer details page which will be used for development
This screen enables user to capture List of Directors/Partners/Trustee/Members manually.
Users can capture details of directors, partners, trustees, or members by selecting a company from the list.
Upon selecting a company, users can opt for 'Print on EB format,' leading to the printing of details in EB format.
User can Generate the CIBIL report for Directors/Partners/Trustee/Members
User can capture the CIBIL Scores of Company/Corporate Guarantor/Personal Guarantor
for List of Directors / Partners/ Trustee / Members
This Screen enables user to capture the CIBIL score and Details of Directors and
This Screen enables user to capture the CIBIL score
This screen allows users to capture the Capital Market Perception/Share Holding Pattern manually.
System will allow to capture Share Holding and Capital Market perception only for Main Borrower.
Additional validation which needs to be performed by system.
This Section is applicable below mentioned Customer Type and Constitution
Constitution: Partnership / Private Limited / Public Limited / Trusts / Limited Liability Partnership / OPC/HUF.
If there are no non-individual customers attached to the application, the corresponding section of the EB format must be automatically filled with the term
( Please refer to the EB format mapping document).
There are 2 functionalities which can be provided.
User to capture the required parameters manually.
If the bank provides API, we can integrate and auto fetch the information.
Note Field will be available as per the requirement of Page. Please refer to the field listing
Field Listing for Share Holding and Capital Market Perception
This section is used to capture and maintain the details of all existing credit exposures of the applicant and associated parties (co-applicant, guarantors, group companies, sister concerns).
The user will be able to fetch
button. This action will trigger an API call to the
to retrieve liability details. If the API call is successful, the liability details will be auto populated on the screen, including
, and other data retrieved from the API. The
is responsible for providing the API for this liability fetch.
In case of API failure, the user must either retry fetching the liability details or enter the details manually.
The user will only be allowed to propose
if the customer already has exposure under other segments such as
We Suggest Treasury Exposure to be fetched here.
But business team has requested in proposed exposure.
Auto-fill or manual entry of the customer name.
Entry of Customer Information File number for unique identification.
Dropdown to select Individual / Corporate / Partnership etc.
Entry or selection of party group (linked customer relationships).
Dropdown to select the applicable sector (e.g., MSME, Retail, MSME).
Dropdown for the type of loan or product (e.g.
Enter the account number of the existing facility.
Dropdown value will consist of Sole/Multiple and Consortium( This will be manual entry since the CBS may not provide this data)
Total limit sanctioned for the facility.
Manual Entry the Banking Arrangement is marked as Multiple and Consortium
Date when the limit was sanctioned.
Interest rate applicable or any concession offered.
Percentage margin to be entered; relevant for secured exposures.
Select from authorized officials responsible for sanction.
Dropdown to choose classification (e.g., Standard, Sub-standard, NPA).
Auto-calculated or manually entered balance due.
The permissible drawing amount based on margin/security.
Free text editor for any comments.
Deviation score or code if applicable.
Days the payment has been overdue.
User will be able to only propose the MSME exposure if the any customer is having exposure like,
Radio button Yes/No to control whether this exposure should be shown
in the recommendation overall exposure will calculated, DOP is considered on the overall exposure
Field Listing and Validation for the Existing Exposure with
Please find the attached field listing same will be considered for the development.
This screen enables the user to
capture the applicant’s existing credit exposures with other financial institutions
). It helps assess total liabilities and calculate accurate eligibility for new proposals.

The data on this screen is automatically fetched from CIBIL, triggered via the Customer 360° page (accessed from the Customer Details section). While the option to manually add or delete records is available, only CIBIL-generated records will be considered for evaluation purposes.
Please find the attached field listing same will be considered for the development.

This section is designed to capture and define the details of the credit facilities proposed for the applicant. It enables the bank to configure loan product parameters, limits, and security classifications for the proposed sanction.

During the Gap Session, the business team suggested that treasury and investment exposures should be considered while determining the DOP and should be included under the proposed exposure. These details will be entered manually by the user.

We will be configuring products named "Treasury Exposure" and "Investment Exposure" in the master setup. At the application level, users will be able to add treasury exposure. Note that Treasury Exposure will not have any ROI or other related parameters.
We also recommend that this should be considered part of Existing Facilities and integrated via API
Field Listing and Validation for the Proposed Exposure
Please find the attached field listing same will be considered for the development.
Documents Details tab allows you to capture the documents for the proposed proposal.
View the list of documents required for the proposed proposal. The document list is pre-configured in the Setup module. Based on the Facility and category selected the documents list is populated.
Field Listing and Validation for the Document details
Please find the attached field listing same will be considered for the development.
Attach security to a facility and map the security ownership to an applicant
Add security to a facility as the primary, secondary or collateral security

The suggested formats for uploading security documents are PDF and Word; however, the system does not restrict other formats such as Excel and text. The size of each document is determined by the parameters allowed by

On the Security Details pop-up, the user needs to select the security type, specify any existing charge, nature of the charge, insurance details, classification, security type, security value, net security, etc. The user can map the security to an applicant. Based on the security type selected, the field structure changes.
database. The new security details display on the main screen.
to enter the Basic details of the security. It is mandatory to enter the basic details for security.

will be triggered and sent to the configured valuer’s email address.

button, a popup screen will appear. The user is required to select the lawyer's name from the dropdown list. On clicking

w:r.w:t: Field Listing and Validation for Security Detail page
Please find the attached field listing same will be considered for the development.
The Security Coverage screen enables users to assess the security coverage provided by applicants for a specific proposal or application.
Please find the security coverage calculation
The system calculates these based on securities attached in the Security Details page.
Primary Security: Typically direct security (e.g., hypothecation of stock).
Collateral Security: Indirect or additional security (e.g., immovable property, third-party guarantee).
Customer Coverage (%): Based on customer-contributed security share.
Summarized section "Details of Security Proposed" for both Primary and Collateral.
"Total Security Coverage" section calculates and displays consolidated figures:
Business Team to confirm on the logics.

This screen empowers users to capture and manage the value of various financial activities, providing a comprehensive overview of the company's earnings. The data captured in this section includes details related to bills purchased/collected, LCs opened, BGs issued, other income (including processing charges/upfront fees), interest, and the total earnings for the present year and the previous year.
We Suggest the Value of account can be fetched directly from the CBS, Bank needs to confirm the API availability.
Field Listing and Validation for Value of account
Please find the attached field listing same will be considered for the development.

. The data entered in this section is intended solely for documentation purposes and will be printed on the

. The data entered in this section is intended solely for documentation purposes and will be printed on the
Field listing is not attached, as the page is static in nature. An EK Editor will be provided for content configuration, and the header will be dynamically fetched from the setup configuration

This screen enables users to capture the exposure norms. During the Gap Session, the business team deliberated that the master data for exposure ceilings should be integrated at the bank level. However, the required API and data touchpoints are to be provided by the bank team. Upon receipt, the same will be reviewed, and necessary changes can be incorporated through CR mode

This screen allows users to enter and maintain information pertaining to both personal and corporate guarantees provided as part of the loan proposal. The captured data supports risk assessment and compliance checks and will be considered during the sanction process.

Field Listing and Validation for the Group Company Operations
This Screen allow the user to enter the current account details of the customer with other banks along with the remarks
Field Listing for Current account with other bank.
When a user initiates the CMA process in the CMA Input Screen, they are prompted to choose the applicable CMA format for the specific application.

Upon selecting the CMA format, the system automatically populates Financial Indicators based on the chosen format. The indicators are displayed year-wise, reflecting the financial health of the borrower over different periods.

The Financial Indicators are dynamic, meaning their calculation is governed by formulas configured in the setup module by administrators. This dynamic nature allows for flexibility and adaptability to changing financial assessment criteria.

The system presents Financial Indicators year-wise, and users have the autonomy to choose which year's indicators should be printed on the ES Format. This selective printing feature enhances customization for reporting and decision-making.

Administrators have the authority to configure formulas for financial ratios and indicators in the setup module. This administrative control ensures that the calculation logic aligns with the specific requirements of each CMA format.
Users can choose the most appropriate CMA format for the application, aligning with the loan type or specific banking guidelines.
Users have the flexibility to decide which year's Financial Indicators should be included in the ES format. This allows for a more tailored and relevant presentation of financial information.
This screen enables the users to capture various required comments for the Financial Indicators.
Based on the Number of Group companies which is added in the customer detail page user can be able to add the financials against each company
Facility Assessment tab allows you to assess the proposal based on various input details in the proposed proposal and the predefined parameters in the setup module.
The following are the assessment types available in the system.
The following are the assessment types available in the system.
will be configured please refer to the attachment below.
will be configured please refer to the attachment below.
will be configured please refer to the attachment below.
will be configured please refer to the attachment below.
Foreign will be configured please refer to the attachment below.
will be configured please refer to the attachment below.
Any additional assessment which can be handled in manual assessment, User needs to enter manually under manual assessment section.
This screen enables the user to capture the Term Loan Assessment, facility-wise, based on both fresh and existing assessments. The screen dynamically updates based on the selected facility
System will allow Multiple Term Loan Assessment and user needs to select the facility name from the dropdown loan
Comments on applicable aspects to be given:
Brief about project (including other infrastructure availabilities), preferably in Bullet Points
Cost of project / means of finance
Description on each item of Cost of project / means of finance
Status of statutory approvals and clearances
Brief on TEV Study (Other Bank / Our Bank) dated ____, wherever applicable
Brief on LIE Study (Other Bank / Our Bank) dated wherever applicable
Basic assumptions for deriving profitability statement
Calculation of DSCR, IRR / BEP Details( , wherever applicable)
Calculation of sensitivity analysis, wherever applicable
Cost of project, Means of finance with
Date of last Lender Independent Engineer Report, wherever applicable and comments thereof.
Implementation schedule vis-à-vis Achievement and Date of commencement of commercial operation wherever applicable and comments thereof.
Comparison of Physical Progress vis-à-vis Financial Progress
Dates of last inspection by branch/offices
Date of Technical Inspection, wherever applicable and comments thereof.
Repayment schedule [Comparison shall be provided as per Repayment Schedule vis-à-vis Amount repaid till date]
Any other matter along with justification
This screen enables user to manually capture the assessment in the mode of
this screen is applicable for smaller loans.
This screen enables users to view the DSCR for the facility, which is being assessed, bank shared DSCR will be configured.
This screen facilitates entry and comparison of internal and external credit ratings for both the
. It enables accurate credit appraisal and compliance tracking by capturing critical rating-related data. The following functionalities are included.
Integration with the ICON rating platform, will be discussed upon receipt of API specification availability.
Field Listing for Internal Rating is below
This Screen enables the user to capture the External rating. Presently this screen is standalone, and the user needs to manually fill in the external rating.
If API is provided, we can Integrate the External Rating.
Please refer to the snapshot of proposed screen.

This Screen enables the users to capture capital cost

RAROC stands for Risk-Adjusted Return on Capital, and it is a financial metric used in the banking and financial industry to evaluate the profitability of a particular investment or business activity while considering the associated risks.
As per the First Phase of SRS we will provide a RAROC as manual entry. Please refer to the fields below for the First Phase delivery
After Tax Risk Adjusted Income (In Crore)
Shortfall, if any, to achieve Hurdle RAROC(In Crore)
Due Diligence of Borrower and Property

screen will allow users to capture and validate information related to the borrower's background and property details. This will ensure that all necessary verifications are performed before sanctioning a proposal.
Field Listing and Validation for Due Diligence for Property
A new screen will be introduced called due diligence of borrower.
A new screen will be introduced called due diligence of borrower. User needs to manually capture the inputs.
Validation for Due Diligence for Borrower
Existing Screen to be considered for list of fields.
Field listing for Pre inspection Report
This section enables users to capture the various compliance related to Proposal and Customer borrowing.

This screen enables the user to capture Yes/No variables for the ES Note, which are configured through the Annexure Setup. Please note that this screen has no system integration; the captured inputs are solely for printing on the ES Note
Please note that the field listing is not attached, as this is a static page. The required fields will be configured from the Annexure Master within the application
This section enables users to other comments for the ES note

Query History tab allows you to view all the queries raised and resolved through the proposal processing stages. The following screen displays. View the queries and the corresponding replies to the queries raised by you and the other users. Click Edit to alter the queries raised by you before it is saved in the system

w:r.w:t: Raise Query allows you to raise a new query upon other uses. Select the recipients, nature of the query, subject, priority, attach any supporting documents, enter a description of your concern. Click
. The query queued to be raised and listed below the action buttons.
Save as Draft: The queries saved as draft will not be visible to other users.
add a new query. Your query will be visible to the other concerned users. These queries can be tracked below
: Select the query saved as a draft. Click Remove Query to permanently delete the query.
Attach relevant documents to your query.

w:r.w:t: The terms and conditions specific to the product and the general terms and conditions are pre-configured in the setup module.
specific to the loan product selected.
The user can add new terms and conditions in addition to the existing terms and conditions. The user must click
at the top left-hand side of the screen to add new terms and conditions. A popup window appears listing the terms and conditions.
select the checkbox corresponding to the terms and conditions and click

button. The selected terms and conditions are added to the Terms and conditions screen in the proposed loan application. The terms and conditions created in the application will apply only to the proposed application. Click the
Adding User Defined Terms and Conditions
In addition to the pre-configured terms and conditions, the user can also manually attach other terms and conditions at the time of application creation. In this scenario, the user needs to select
from the popup window and then click Take
button. The terms and conditions get attached as a draft. However, it is editable. The user can rename the terms and conditions.
Terms and conditions are configurable by bank team. We will map according to the requirements.
The terms and conditions attached in the application will be applied only to the proposed application
The terms & conditions will be configured as per Office Note.
The user can add new terms and conditions by clicking at the top left-hand side of the screen. A popup window appears listing the terms and conditions.
The user needs to select the checkbox corresponding to the terms and conditions and click
button. The selected terms and conditions are added to the Terms and conditions screen in the proposed loan application. Click the
The terms and conditions attached in the application will be applied only to the proposed application.
In addition to the pre-configured terms and conditions, the user can also manually attach other terms and conditions at the time of recommendation.
In this scenario, the user needs to select
from the popup window and then click the
button. The terms and conditions are attached as a draft. However, it is editable. The user can edit the terms and conditions.
terms & conditions, the user needs to click at the top left-hand side of the screen to add new terms and conditions. A popup window appears listing the terms and conditions.
Select the checkbox corresponding to the terms and conditions to be attached to the proposal and then click the

button. The selected terms and conditions are added to the Terms and conditions screen in the proposed loan application. The terms and conditions created in the application will apply only to the proposed application.
This screen enables users to capture the concession on the Interest, processing fee, Commission and any other fee which are requested for concession.
Interest, Processing Fee, Commission, and Other Fees
: Ensure that users can only enter valid numbers (System will allow user to capture the value of concession places).
: The system will refer to the interest rate, if any, from the "Existing Exposure with
: The system will refer to the configured rate for the facility (this is derived from the setup).
: The user needs to enter the concession proposed for each facility.
: This is a system-calculated field. The system will calculate the proposed percentage based on the difference between the card rate and the concession requested.
: This is an auto-calculated field. Card rate – Concession = Proposed Processing Charge.
Field Listing and Validation for pricing page/business approval
Please find the attached field listing same will be considered for the development.
screen allows the user to manage the product/user defined deviations while processing the loan application, starting from raising the deviation through deviation approval.
The following two types of deviation can be handled in this screen.
The user defined deviations are that which are manually raised by the user on this screen.
button allows the user to raise multiple deviations as required.
field is mandatory to save the

User defined should be pre-configured in setup by the system admin and it can be selected by the user and recommend for deviation. For any new scenario, the user has to contact the admin to configure the
Deviation will be configured based on available global variables only.
The product defined deviations are automatically raised by the system if any of the input parameter breaches from the
or the setup benchmark. The system displays the deviated parameter and the corresponding minimum approving authority on this screen.
from the drop-down and type the
The loan can be sanctioned only after the deviation is approved by the respective authorized authority.
It is limitation, We will come back upon discussion with ou
The minimum approving authority will be automatically displayed for the raised deviation. The underwriter needs to enter the recommendation
. The user needs to forward the proposal by navigating to
screen. In the Workflow & Approval screen, select the respective deviation flow point and select the user from the
drop-down and send the proposal to the respective approving authority.
, the approving authority can pick the proposal for approving the deviations. In this stage, the entire proposal will be in the read-only mode except the
The approving authority can review the deviation, approve/reject the deviation by selecting the respective value from the
After approving the deviation, the approver needs to send back the proposal by navigating to
screen. In the Workflow & Approval screen, select the next workflow point and the respective user group and send the application to process further.
The system will not allow to sanction until all the deviations are approved. Sanction is restricted even any one of the deviations is rejected.
It is limitation, We will come back upon discussion with our product team.
screen allows the authorized user to recommend the proposal for sanction.
On this screen, the system displays the
button, certain fields whichever applicable becomes editable in the Recommendation
table. When the recommending authority is modifying the recommended amount then the sanction amount will also get updated.
Once the proposal is forwarded to the approving authority, the approving authority will review the proposal and change the
should not breach the loan amount requested.
In both the above cases, the loan cannot be sanctioned until the deviation is approved by the
It is limitation, We will come back upon discussion with our product team.
For sanctioning authority, the sanction amount can’t be greater than recommended amount and
authority, final sanction amount cannot exceed the recommended amount.
, the system automatically displays the respective approving authority groups in the

dropdown. The user can choose other appropriate approving authority groups which is higher than the minimum approving authority displayed based on the proposal condition and enter the recommended remarks in the
The user can also edit the recommended remarks when required by selecting the user’s name in the

The user can edit only their remarks until the proposal is within their inbox. Once the proposal is forwarded to the next workflow, the system will restrict the user from editing the recommendation remarks.
System will identify the Actual DOA based on the application captured data, as per the DOA which is
we have identified the below mentioned global variable for deriving the DOA.
The SL Preparation screen allows the user to create the Sanction Letter.
The pre-filled content is dynamically fetched from a
configured in the backend or admin panel.
, the corresponding sanction letter template is selected.
View and modify the contents before finalizing.
Add remarks, loan terms, or approval conditions if needed.
The SL Generation screen allows the user to view the Sanction Letter and the annexure to the Sanction Letter. On this screen,
screen allows the user to view the final sanction letter for the primary borrower attached to the proposal.
The user can view the Sanction letter details for the selected proposal. The user must click the
button to view the Sanction letter for the proposed proposal in a pop-up window.
Upon approval of a proposal, the “
” buttons on the SL Generation screen will be disabled.
The Workflow & Approval screen allows the user to track the workflow for recommendation/approval.
displays the list of mandatory pages preconfigured against workflow. If mandatory screen is completed, a green tick mark will be shown beside the page name. Else a red cross mark will be shown.
In case if any of the mandatory screen is incomplete, the system will restrict the user from forwarding the application to the next stage.
To forward the proposal to the approving authority, the user needs to select the workflow of the approving authority and the approving authority/group from the respective dropdowns, and then click
. The proposal is forwarded to the Group Inbox or the approving authority inbox based on the selection in the
Once the approving authority receives the proposal from the user, the approving authority can either approve, reject, withdraw, or undo the proposal by clicking the respective buttons on this screen.
While approving, the approver can give comments. The comments section is editable only for approver.
The Approve, Reject, and Undo buttons are enabled only for the approving authority.

– The approving authority needs to click this button to send back the proposal to the previous workflow. Once the proposal is sent back, the user can revisit the sanction condition and sanction amount. Once the proposal is sent back, the entire proposal becomes editable.
In this scenario, if any of the assessment and score related parameters gets changed, the eligibility should be revisited and re-sanctioned with revised parameters and data.

– The button available at all levels. Upon clicked, the proposal will be removed from the processing inbox. However, it can be searched in the existing proposal search. In this scenario, the proposal will be in
To forward the proposal, the user needs to
select the workflow and user from the

. The proposal is forwarded to the repective workflow either to the Inbox of the selected user or the group. This process is common to forward the proposal for recommendation, for sanction, for deviation, and to send back.
Fort the proposal which needs to be sent to the committee
powers of the bank will be complied and configured in the system.

w:r.w:t: If the DOA is CAC then the consise note will be generated, and user needs to add the subjective information in the Business updates,Same way for ES 18 and E19s.
If any deviation is present, the user needs to send the proposal for deviation approval by using respective deviation workflow.

Once the proposal is sent to the approving authority and the approving authority will receive the proposal other screens in a read-only mode and perform necessary action only in the Deviation Tracking screen, Proposal Recommendation screen, and Workflow & Approval screen.
The application will verify DOP of the proposal if matching then the

button will be enabled. Once the proposal is approved, then the Approve, Reject and Withdraw button will be disabled and Undo button will be enabled. The sanctioning authority will be automatically enabled based on the DOP shared.
: For Deviation, User needs to manually Select the respective workflow and get the approval.
screen allows the user to view the completed workflow points with the comments entered by various users along with the time stamp.
This screen enables users to add post sanction documentation
Proposed Solution for Post Sanction Documentation Flow. Detailed information is explained in the Manual Documents and DDE

w:r.w:t: Annexure will be provided since documentation across
Once the proposal is approved by the sanctioning authority, the user can
to create a unique loan account number for the proposal in the CBS.
Once the loan account is created in CBS, it will be
The loan account number is auto populated on the relevant field on the
CGTMSE coverage. New Button will be introduced in the Account Hand off screen called Push data to CGTMSE. This button will be enables once the account loan account is created.
